description: A face in a crowd may become familiar before its name returns. That first connection is 'miratu': a present perception or proposition matches something already known. 'halemu' (remember) brings earlier knowledge to mind; 'shelomu' (understand) grasps what it means or how it fits with other things.
articulatory_notes: Close the lips for voiced nasal 'm', carry the voice through the nose, and release into high-front unrounded 'i'. Tap or briefly trill the tongue at the alveolar ridge before stressed open central 'a'. Place the tongue tip against the back of the upper teeth for dental 't' and release into high-back rounded 'u'.
usage_notes: 'miratu' takes the recognised material directly. For a proposition, 'mena ... meno' supplies the complement frame. In noun position, 'miratu' is recognition. 'thekao' (acknowledge) is the separate act of explicitly responding to a presence or communication.

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Recognising someone says that this is the same person encountered before. Their thoughts and character remain their own.

semantic_domains.cognition: a present perception or proposition connected with prior knowledge
